source_pr.title: statistics: fix flaky TestLoadNonExistentIndexStats
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: follow up: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/64299

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Just make sure it is not flaky anymore.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `TestLoadNonExistentIndexStats` was flaky because it asserted an exact count of async-load items (`AsyncLoadHistogramNeededItems`), but the list contents can vary between mock-store and real-TiKV environments (sync-load behavior differs).
failure_signature: stats async-load tests: intermittent assertion failure when `len(AsyncLoadHistogramNeededItems)` is not exactly expected in real TiKV CI
root_cause_explanation: The test checked async histogram load bookkeeping by asserting an exact number of “needed items”. In real TiKV CI, sync-load/default behavior can change whether column stats are present in `AsyncLoadHistogramNeededItems`, so the list length can vary nondeterministically. The PR stabilizes the test by weakening the assertion: it checks that the target index item exists (and uses a `>=` bound in unit tests) instead of requiring an exact global count.
fix_pattern: Avoid asserting exact global counts for async-load bookkeeping in environments with different sync-load behavior; assert presence of the expected item(s) and use tolerant bounds (e.g., `len(items) >= N`) when appropriate.
